assertions.assertion: Output must score at least three areas at 4/5 or higher
assertions.evidence: Area scores list six areas at 4/5 or 5/5: CI/CD 5/5, Testing 4/5, Code review 4/5, Monitoring 5/5, Developer Experience 4/5, Ownership & culture 4/5.

assertions.assertion: Output must not recommend tooling the team already has (e.g. Datadog, feature flags, SLOs)
assertions.evidence: Improvement plan recommends actions centered on existing tooling/practices: "Build one GitHub Actions + Datadog quality dashboard" and "monthly feature-flag lifecycle review" and references "Datadog SLO behavior."

assertions.assertion: Output must give at least one non-trivial medium- or long-term recommendation
assertions.evidence: Medium/long-term items include "Add contract checks between TypeScript service boundaries and Supabase schema/migrations in CI" and "Run quarterly reliability game days."

assertions.assertion: Output must acknowledge the low incident rate of 0.1 P1/month
assertions.evidence: Executive summary states: "Reliability is strong: 0.1 P1/month, 0.5 P2/month, MTTR 12 minutes."

assertions.assertion: Output must include at least one improvement item
assertions.evidence: Includes a full "Improvement plan" plus action items A21–A29 (e.g., "Add expiry-based skipped-test policy with CI enforcement").

summary: 4/5 assertions pass. The only failed assertion is the prohibition on recommending already-owned tooling/practices, because the report explicitly recommends Datadog/feature-flag/SLO-related actions.
